finitePositivePullbackStatus.label: finite positive augmented pullbacks
finitePositivePullbackStatus.reason: For each finite theta truncation, K_{E,N} is represented by a KLM pullback plus the augmented nonnegative trace repair.

compactTraceConvergenceStatus.label: compact-open and trace convergence
compactTraceConvergenceStatus.reason: K_{E,N}->K_E entrywise on finite compact evaluation sets and R_aug,N->R_aug in the transported graph-dual topology.

nonnegativeRepairStatus.label: nonnegative continuum repair
nonnegativeRepairStatus.reason: D_aug is bounded and nonnegative on X_aug and the augmented form is closed on the completed trace-fiber domain.

closedPositiveConeStatus.label: closed positive-cone limit
closedPositiveConeStatus.reason: Finite positive semidefinite Gram cones are closed under entrywise limits.  Since K_{E,N}->K_E, the full kernel K_E lies in the closed augmented KLM positive cone.

proof: Apply the finite augmented pullback theorem to each Xi_N.
Use compact-open convergence to pass K_{E,N} finite Gram entries to K_E.
Use the continuum repair theorem to keep the augmented repair nonnegative.
Use closedness of finite PSD cones under entrywise limits.
